title: Hytale Server Economy: Why Most Operators Get It Wrong
author: Veltrix
excerpt: The default economy configuration ships with flat pricing. That single decision kills long-term player retention. Here is what to do instead.

content.text: Every new Hytale server starts with the same mistake. The admin shop sells iron for two coins and buys it back for one. That spread never changes. On day one it feels fair. By week three it has destroyed the player economy entirely.

When prices never move, players find the highest-margin item, farm it infinitely, and flood the market. Currency accumulates at the top. New players cannot compete. Retention collapses between weeks three and five — every time.

A dynamic pricing engine watches buy and sell volume per item. When players sell more iron than they buy, the sell price drops automatically. When demand spikes, buy prices rise. The market self-corrects without operator intervention.
The goal is not realism. The goal is to make farming any single item less efficient than diversifying — which is exactly what keeps players engaged past week four.

Three values control everything: sensitivity (how fast prices move), floor (minimum sell price), and ceiling (maximum buy price). Most operators set sensitivity too high. Prices swing wildly. Players panic. Set it low. Let it breathe.
Dynamic Pricing Engine is available in the Veltrix Premium catalog.
